title: The Kill Test — What HUF Cannot Do, Must Not Do, and Will Fail At
classification: FOUNDATIONAL — negative control on the framework itself
citation: Peter Higgins / Rogue Wave Audio, with the HUF AI Collective: Claude (Anthropic), ChatGPT (OpenAI), Copilot (Microsoft), Gemini (Google), Grok (xAI)

why_this_document_exists.peter_formulation: Just for my sanity, do a what HUF can't do, just to make sure it is real. Sort of make a kill HUF worst failure test of things never to do, make sure it really fails.
why_this_document_exists.purpose: If HUF cannot be broken, it is not real science. Every valid framework has a boundary where it stops working. This document maps that boundary. If ONTO-001 is the ground HUF stands on, KILL-001 is the cliff edge where the ground ends. Both are required for honest assessment.
why_this_document_exists.design_principle: This is T6 — the negative control — applied to HUF itself. The framework that built the calibration rack must pass its own rack. If HUF claims to measure structural dynamics in proportional systems, there must be conditions where it definitively does NOT work, and those conditions must be documented with the same rigor as the successes.

category_1_WRONG_DATA.title: Feed HUF the Wrong Kind of Data — It Will Produce Garbage

failures.name: Non-proportional data
failures.description: HUF requires data that sums to 1 (or 100%). Feed it absolute quantities — GDP in dollars, temperature in Celsius, population counts — and every metric is meaningless. K_eff computed on absolute numbers is not effective dimensionality. TV computed on non-simplex data is not structural velocity. The math will execute. The numbers will appear. They will mean nothing.
failures.example: Compute K_eff on [5000, 12000, 8500, 3200] (raw revenue by department). The math runs. The answer is wrong. These are not proportions. They do not sit on a simplex. The unit-sum constraint does not apply. Everything downstream is fiction.
failures.evidence: T6c (non-ratio fake simplex) proved this: bad carrier produces meaningless metrics. HUF correctly fails when the simplex condition is violated.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED KILL — HUF is dead on arrival with non-proportional data

failures.name: Too few carrier elements
failures.description: With 2 carrier elements, the simplex is a line segment. K_eff ranges from 1 to 2. TV is a single number. There is almost no structural information. With 3 elements (GDP sectors: Agriculture/Industry/Services), the simplex is a triangle. K_eff range is 1 to 3. Sensitivity is low. Meaningful structural analysis requires enough carrier elements to exhibit real dimensionality variation.
failures.example: GDP sector composition with 3 carriers showed statistically significant results (Japan p=0.0098) but with much lower sensitivity than finance (9 carriers) or EMBER (9 carriers). At 2 carriers, the framework is essentially a single ratio — it degenerates to a trivial measurement.
failures.threshold: Below 3 carriers: not recommended. 3-4 carriers: limited sensitivity, use with caution. 5+ carriers: normal operating range. 9+ carriers: full sensitivity.
failures.kill_status: DEGRADED — not dead but progressively blinded as carrier count drops

failures.name: Carrier elements that don't cover the system
failures.description: If the carrier elements are a partial list — if they sum to less than 100% because significant components are omitted or lumped into 'Other' — the simplex is distorted. An 'Other' category that absorbs 30% of the system masks the dynamics of whatever is inside it. Garbage in, governance out.
failures.example: Energy mix with [Coal, Gas, Nuclear, Other] where 'Other' contains solar, wind, hydro, and biomass at 35% combined. The four renewable sources have rich internal dynamics that are invisible because they are compressed into a single carrier. K_eff is artificially low. Drift in renewable composition is undetectable.
failures.evidence: T2a (Planck carrier revision) proved that carrier construction is the first-order validation gate. Wrong carrier = wrong everything. Right carrier = everything works.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED KILL — HUF with bad carrier construction is worse than no analysis, because it looks legitimate

failures.name: Single time point
failures.description: HUF measures dynamics — change over time. Give it a single snapshot and it can compute K_eff (how spread out it is right now) but nothing else. No velocity. No drift. No forward signal. No shock detection. A photograph is not a movie. One reading is not a time series.
failures.kill_status: PARTIAL KILL — K_eff survives, everything else dies

category_2_WRONG_QUESTION.title: Ask HUF the Wrong Question — It Will Give You a Confident Wrong Answer
category_2_WRONG_QUESTION.severity: CRITICAL — more dangerous than data failure because the output looks plausible

failures.name: Why is this happening?
failures.description: HUF tells you WHAT the structure is doing. It does not tell you WHY. K_eff is dropping — why? HUF has no idea. Maybe it's policy change. Maybe it's market forces. Maybe it's seasonal. Maybe it's a data error. The instrument reads the state. Causation is the operator's job. If anyone claims HUF explains causation, they have closed the loop and violated LOOP-001.
failures.example: German electricity K_eff drops from 7.2 to 5.1 between 2015 and 2020. HUF reports: concentration increasing, coal declining, renewables not fully replacing coal's proportional contribution. HUF does NOT report: this is because of the Energiewende policy. The policy inference is the human's. The instrument just reads the gauge.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED BOUNDARY — HUF is causally blind by design

failures.name: Will this specific event happen?
failures.description: HUF does not predict events. The forward signal detects a CONDITION — structural concentration building behind apparent stability. That condition has preceded shocks in three domains. But the condition does not guarantee a shock. And the absence of the condition does not guarantee safety. And the timing of any future shock is unknown. HUF sees the barometric pressure dropping. It does not know when the storm arrives or whether it will arrive at all.
failures.example: Deceptive drift detected in Japan GDP composition (p=0.0098). This does NOT mean 'Japan will have an economic crisis.' It means 'Japan's GDP sector proportions are concentrating in a pattern that has historically preceded structural disruption.' The disruption might not come. The pattern might resolve. The system might redistribute without a shock. The instrument reads the condition, not the future.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED BOUNDARY — HUF detects conditions, not events

failures.name: What should I do about this?
failures.description: HUF does not recommend action. Ever. This is LOOP-001 — the Skydiver Principle. The instrument shows the altitude. It does not say jump. If HUF is used to generate recommendations, automated alerts, policy prescriptions, or investment decisions, it has been misused. The output may look actionable. It is not action. It is observation. Anyone who treats HUF output as instruction has turned the altimeter into a hand pushing them out of the plane.
failures.kill_status: DOCTRINAL KILL — this is not a limitation, it is a design requirement

failures.name: Is this system healthy?
failures.description: HUF does not define health. K_eff declining might be healthy (natural consolidation after a growth phase) or unhealthy (monoculture taking over a diverse ecosystem). TV spiking might be healthy (rapid adaptation to a new condition) or unhealthy (chaotic destabilization). The instrument does not carry a model of 'good' or 'bad.' The operator brings domain context. Without it, HUF readings are structurally accurate and semantically empty.
failures.example: A wetland's K_eff drops from 6.1 to 4.3 over five years. Is this degradation or natural succession? HUF cannot tell you. An ecologist can. The ecologist needs HUF to see the structural change. HUF needs the ecologist to interpret it.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED BOUNDARY — HUF is value-neutral by design

failures.name: What's happening to this specific element?
failures.description: HUF resolves at the carrier level, not below it. Identity resolution (APP-001) means the framework tracks categories, not individuals. It sees 'coal declining from 40% to 30%' but not which specific coal plant closed. It sees 'Model A growing from 12% to 18%' but not which specific drives were deployed. Sub-carrier dynamics are invisible. This is by design — the simplex operates on proportions of categories — but it means HUF cannot answer questions about individual entities within a carrier element.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED BOUNDARY — sub-carrier resolution does not exist

category_3_WRONG_DOMAIN.title: Apply HUF to Domains Where It Has No Business — It Will Look Like It Works
category_3_WRONG_DOMAIN.severity: DANGEROUS — this is the most insidious failure mode

failures.name: Binary systems
failures.description: A system with two states (on/off, alive/dead, pass/fail) is not a proportional system in any useful sense. You can force it onto a simplex [p, 1-p] but K_eff ranges from 1 to 2 and TV is a single number. The framework adds nothing that a simple percentage doesn't already provide. Applying HUF here is not wrong — it is useless. It is a sledgehammer for a thumbtack.
failures.kill_status: USELESSNESS KILL — technically valid, practically worthless

failures.name: Externally forced systems
failures.description: HUF's forward signal detects INTERNAL structural concentration. It explicitly MISSES external forcing events. Fukushima — external. COVID in most countries — external. Oil spills — external. Storms — external. If a domain's primary dynamics are driven by external shocks rather than internal redistribution, HUF's most distinctive capability (deceptive drift detection) is blind to the thing that matters most.
failures.evidence: TRIAD-FS v2.0 documented this explicitly: 'External shocks (Fukushima, France nuclear crisis, COVID for most countries) consistently MISS — the signal detects INTERNAL structural concentration, not external forcing.'
failures.example: Using HUF to monitor earthquake preparedness by tracking building-type proportions. An earthquake is an external event. HUF will not see it coming. If the building stock is quietly concentrating toward one structural type (internal drift), HUF might detect that. But the earthquake itself — the thing you actually care about — is invisible.
failures.kill_status: CONFIRMED KILL — HUF is structurally blind to external forcing

failures.name: Systems where the carrier is artificial
failures.description: If the proportional categories are arbitrary — imposed by the analyst rather than reflecting real system structure — then the simplex is an artifact and HUF measures the artifact, not the system. This is T6c applied broadly: you can always create fake proportions from any data, but the dynamics of those proportions reflect the categorization scheme, not the underlying reality.
failures.example: Arbitrarily dividing a company's activities into 5 categories and tracking their revenue share. If the categories don't correspond to real structural divisions — if 'Category 3' lumps unrelated activities that happen to share a budget line — then K_eff and TV are measuring the categorization, not the company. The analyst built the simplex. The system didn't.
failures.kill_status: INSIDIOUS KILL — the output looks legitimate, the foundation is sand

failures.name: Small sample, high noise
failures.description: HUF metrics are computed from data. Noisy data with few time points produces noisy metrics. K_eff jitters. TV spikes from noise, not signal. Deceptive drift detection requires enough time points to establish a baseline and enough signal-to-noise ratio to distinguish real concentration from random fluctuation. The framework's statistical tests (p-values, split-sample validation) exist precisely because raw metrics lie when the data is thin.
failures.example: Five annual surveys of a wetland's habitat composition. K_eff shows a downward trend. Is it real? With five points, you cannot distinguish trend from noise. You cannot split-sample validate. You cannot deseasonalize. The instrument is reading, but the reading is not trustworthy.
failures.kill_status: DEGRADED KILL — framework operates but confidence is zero

category_4_WRONG_USE.title: Use HUF for Things It Was Designed Not to Do — It Will Become Dangerous
category_4_WRONG_USE.severity: EXISTENTIAL — these violate the framework's foundational doctrine

failures.name: Close the loop
failures.description: Connect HUF output to an automated response system. Alert triggers when K_eff crosses a threshold. Policy changes when TV exceeds a limit. Trading algorithms act on deceptive drift signals. The instrument becomes a controller. The altimeter becomes the hand. The skydiver didn't jump — they were pushed. This is not a failure of HUF. This is a failure of the human who removed themselves from the loop. But the outcome is the same: the framework is now making decisions about things it does not understand, for people who did not consent, based on thresholds nobody voted on.
failures.kill_status: DOCTRINAL KILL — violates LOOP-001, Skydiver Principle, ONTO-001

failures.name: Overclaim the evidence
failures.description: State that HUF 'predicts crises.' State that deceptive drift 'validates across all domains.' State that the forward signal is 'proven.' Each of these exceeds the standing assessment. The forward signal is CONDITIONAL. Non-finance domains are single-country findings. Split-sample validation exists for finance only. The headline rule applies: never claim more than HSA-001 licenses. Overclaiming is how frameworks die — not from being wrong, but from being right about less than they say.
failures.evidence: ChatGPT flagged this in the whitepaper: 'validated across multiple domains' was cooled to 'tested across multiple domains with conditional forward-signal evidence.' The correction was appropriate.
failures.kill_status: CREDIBILITY KILL — the framework survives, the reputation does not

failures.name: Skip carrier validation
failures.description: Load data into the analyzer without checking whether the carrier elements are valid, complete, and meaningful. Trust the output because the math ran. This is the most common failure mode in any analytical framework and HUF is not immune. CL-01 (the validity gate) exists precisely because everything downstream depends on the carrier being real. Skip CL-01 and you get T6c: garbage metrics that look professional.
failures.kill_status: OPERATIONAL KILL — the instrument is uncalibrated, every reading is suspect

failures.name: Apply it to a domain without domain expertise
failures.description: An analyst with HUF but no ecological training monitors a wetland. K_eff is dropping. TV is calm. Deceptive drift pattern detected. The analyst reports: 'the wetland is degrading.' But the ecologist knows: this is a seasonal pattern. Or: this species displacement is natural succession. Or: the K_eff drop is driven by a monitoring methodology change, not a real ecological shift. HUF without domain expertise is a precise instrument in untrained hands. The readings are accurate. The interpretation is wrong.
failures.kill_status: INTERPRETATION KILL — the instrument works, the operator fails

failures.name: Treat it as the only instrument
failures.description: HUF measures structural dynamics of proportional systems. It does not measure water quality, species health, financial solvency, grid reliability, or any other domain-specific indicator. It is one instrument among many. A doctor does not diagnose with only a thermometer. An ecologist does not assess with only K_eff. HUF is a structural layer that complements existing domain monitoring — it does not replace it. Using HUF as the sole instrument for any decision is malpractice.
failures.kill_status: COMPLETENESS KILL — HUF sees one layer, domains have many layers

category_5_FUNDAMENTAL_LIMITS.title: Things HUF Will Never Do, No Matter How Much the Framework Develops
category_5_FUNDAMENTAL_LIMITS.severity: PERMANENT — these are mathematical limits, not engineering gaps

failures.name: Measure absolute quantities
failures.description: HUF operates on proportions. Proportions destroy magnitude information. A system where coal is 40% of the energy mix looks the same to HUF whether total energy is 100 TWh or 100,000 TWh. If total system size matters — and it often does — HUF cannot see it. Two wetlands with identical carrier proportions but different total hectares are indistinguishable. This is the simplex constraint: everything sums to 1, so scale vanishes.
failures.kill_status: MATHEMATICAL LIMIT — proportions cannot encode magnitude

failures.name: Detect sub-carrier dynamics
failures.description: If 'renewables' is a carrier element at 25%, HUF sees 25%. It does not see that solar is growing from 8% to 15% while wind is declining from 12% to 7% within that 25%. Sub-carrier redistribution is invisible unless the carrier is reconstructed at finer resolution. This is not a bug — it is the identity resolution principle. But it means real dynamics can hide inside a carrier element.
failures.kill_status: MATHEMATICAL LIMIT — the simplex resolves at the carrier level only

failures.name: Distinguish between causes of the same structural signature
failures.description: K_eff declining + TV calm = deceptive drift. But the cause could be: natural consolidation, deliberate policy, market failure, measurement artifact, seasonal cycle, or genuine danger. The structural signature is identical for all six. HUF sees the pattern. It cannot see which of the six is operating. Every interpretation requires domain knowledge external to the framework.
failures.kill_status: MATHEMATICAL LIMIT — structural signatures are degenerate with respect to causation

failures.name: Guarantee a future shock from deceptive drift
failures.description: Deceptive drift precedes shocks in the empirical record (3 domains, 4 significant findings). But the conditional probability is not 1. Not every period of quiet concentration produces a subsequent shock. Some systems concentrate and then gradually redistribute without a crisis. The forward signal is a conditional indicator, not a deterministic prediction. It will produce false positives. The false positive rate is not yet measured for non-finance domains.
failures.kill_status: STATISTICAL LIMIT — conditional signal, not deterministic prediction

failures.name: Validate itself from inside
failures.description: HUF cannot prove HUF is correct using HUF. The framework requires external validation — independent domains, independent data, independent analysts — to establish that its measurements correspond to reality. The calibration rack exists precisely because self-referential validation is not validation. This is why the triad methodology demands three independent domains: because the framework must be checked against the world, not against itself.
failures.kill_status: EPISTEMOLOGICAL LIMIT — no framework can be its own evidence

the_honest_score.worst_failure_mode: KILL-3.3 (artificial carrier) — because the output looks completely legitimate. Every other kill mode is detectable by a competent operator. An artificial carrier produces professional-looking metrics from a meaningless foundation. The framework itself cannot detect this failure. Only the domain expert can. This is why KILL-4.4 (no domain expertise) is the operational twin of KILL-3.3 — together they form the most dangerous combination: artificial carrier + no domain expert = confident, precise, and completely wrong.
the_honest_score.overall_assessment: HUF is real. It has hard boundaries, confirmed failure modes, and mathematical limits that cannot be engineered away. The boundaries are where they should be for a framework that measures structural dynamics of proportional systems. The failures are what you would expect: wrong data, wrong questions, wrong domains, wrong use. None of the kill conditions undermine the core claim (ONTO-001) — they define its scope. A framework with no kill conditions would be unfalsifiable. HUF has 19. That is the sanity check.

peter_sanity_verdict.statement: If HUF survived this document intact — if none of these 19 conditions collapse the ontological claim, the core metrics, or the calibration rack — then the framework is real. Not because it works everywhere. Because it fails where it should, for reasons that are structural, documented, and honest.
peter_sanity_verdict.for_peterson: A framework that cannot name its own failures is not a scientific framework. KILL-001 names 19.
peter_sanity_verdict.for_ramsar: Before trusting HUF for wetland monitoring, check: are your carrier elements real (not KILL-1.3)? Do you have enough of them (not KILL-1.2)? Do you have enough time points (not KILL-3.4)? Do you have an ecologist interpreting the output (not KILL-4.4)? If yes to all four, the instrument is calibrated for your domain. If no to any of them, the instrument will lie to you with a straight face.
